# FilesHub — full LLM context

> FilesHub (https://fileshub.zaions.com) is a zero-cost file-storage and developer-utility
> API built on Laravel + Nova. This file gives an AI assistant enough detail to answer
> integration questions accurately without crawling every page. Last updated: 2026-06-23.

## What FilesHub is

A small HTTP API that stores files for you and serves them back at a stable URL, plus a
large catalogue of stateless developer utilities. Apps use it as their upload backend so
they never have to run their own storage server. Every request is authenticated with a
per-project API key sent in the `X-API-Key` header. The API is versioned under `/api/v1`.

Base URL: `https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1`

## Authentication

Send `X-API-Key: <key>` on every request. Keys belong to a project and carry permissions:

- `read` — list and download (including private) objects.
- `write` — upload and delete objects.

Keys may be `live` (`fh_live_...`) or `test` (`fh_test_...`). Restricted keys additionally
validate the request origin: browsers send `Origin`/`Referer` (checked against an allowed
domain list); mobile apps send `X-App-Id` with the bundle id and a platform user-agent.
Create and manage keys in the Nova admin panel of your FilesHub deployment.

## The object lifecycle (storage API)

### Upload — POST /api/v1/objects (write)

Multipart `form-data`:

- `file` (required) — the file. Validated against the configured max size
  (`FILESHUB_MAX_UPLOAD_MB`, default 10 MB) and an optional allowed-MIME list.
- `visibility` (optional) — `public` or `private`. Defaults to `private` server-side, but
  the Aoneahsan/Zaions integration convention is to send `public`.
- `expires_in_days` (optional, 1–3650) — auto-delete N days from now.
- `expires_at` (optional, ISO-8601, must be in the future) — auto-delete at an absolute time.
  If both are given, `expires_at` wins.

Success `201`:

```json
{
  "public_id": "01JDKQXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX",
  "project_id": "01JDKPXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX",
  "visibility": "public",
  "mime_type": "application/pdf",
  "size_bytes": 1048576,
  "url": "https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1/objects/01JDKQXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX",
  "expires_at": null
}
```

The `url` is what you store in your own database and render in your UI. The `public_id` is a
ULID — sortable, URL-safe, collision-resistant.

### Download / view — GET /api/v1/objects/{public_id}

Streams the file with its real `Content-Type` and original filename. Public objects need no
key (use the URL directly in an `<img>` or `<a>`); private objects require a `read` key from
the same project. Expired objects return `404` and are cleaned up on access.

### List — GET /api/v1/objects (read)

Query params: `page` (default 1), `per_page` (default 20, max 100), `visibility`
(`public`|`private`, optional filter). Returns:

```json
{
  "data": [
    {
      "public_id": "01JDKQ...",
      "original_filename": "photo.jpg",
      "mime_type": "image/jpeg",
      "size_bytes": 524288,
      "visibility": "public",
      "url": "https://fileshub.zaions.com/api/v1/objects/01JDKQ...",
      "created_at": "2026-06-23T12:00:00+00:00"
    }
  ],
  "meta": { "current_page": 1, "per_page": 20, "total": 150 }
}
```

### Delete — DELETE /api/v1/objects/{public_id} (write)

Permanently deletes the database record and the stored file (a model event removes the file).
Returns `200 { "message": "Deleted" }`. Only objects belonging to the key's project are
deletable.

## Error envelope

Errors return a JSON body `{ "message": "..." }` with an appropriate status code:

- `401` — missing/invalid key, or a private object requested without a key.
- `403` — key lacks the required permission, or origin/app-id not allowed.
- `404` — object not found, expired, or the stored file is missing.
- `422` — validation failed (e.g. file too large, bad `visibility`). The message is the first
  validation error.
- `429` — rate limit exceeded.
- `500` — server error.

## Beyond storage: platform services

FilesHub also exposes 50+ stateless utility endpoints under `/api/v1`, including: hashing and
verification, Base64/URL encoding, JSON/CSV/XML/YAML converters, UUID/ULID/NanoID generators,
QR codes, short URLs, OTP send/verify, an encryption vault, image resize/compress/crop/convert,
PDF generation, link previews, sitemap/feed generation, webhooks, uptime monitors, and scheduled
tasks. These share the same `X-API-Key` auth and base URL. The storage object API above is the
part most apps integrate first.

## Honest limits

- Single-region, locally-stored backend — not a global CDN. Use a CDN for large global media.
- Default 10 MB upload cap (configurable per deployment).
- No published client SDK yet; integrate over plain HTTP in any language. A TypeScript SDK is
  archived until the API stabilises.

## Author

https://github.com/aoneahsan · https://www.npmjs.com/~aoneahsan
